verbGlossary.ADDS SHIFTS OF: {source} acceleration adds overtime shifts or second-shift crews of {target} to compress activity durations
verbGlossary.ANALYZES IMPACT OF: {source}quantifies {target} effect of a resource delay (equipment breakdown or material late delivery)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source}is assigned to {target} as a required crew member for execution
verbGlossary.ASSESSES LOADING OF: {source}checks whether resource loading on activities meets {target} quality thresholds
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZES DELAY OF: {source}identifies a resource or material shortage as the cause of {target} delay event
verbGlossary.COMMITTED IN: {source} foreman commits their crew to specific activities in the look-ahead or Last Planner weekly work plan
verbGlossary.COMMITS: {source}Planner System secures reliable promises from trade foremen to complete specific activities in the upcoming week
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINED BY: {source}delivery window imposes {target} on when an activity can start
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: A limited or shared resource (e.g., tower crane) constrains which activities can proceed simultaneously on {target}
verbGlossary.COST-LOADS: Cost loading assigns the dollar value of a resource (labor rate, equipment rental, material cost) to {target} activities for cash flow projection
verbGlossary.DELIVERED FOR: {source}is delivered to site on {target}d date to support a specific construction activity
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: {source} capacity or haul cycle time determines the achievable production rate and thus {target} duration
verbGlossary.DRIVEN BY: {target} is driven by availability or lead time of a key resource that gates downstream activities
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source}directly drives {target} because its delivery or availability sets the earliest possible start
verbGlossary.EXTENDS DUE TO: {source}is filed because {target} delivery delay pushed activities beyond contractual completion
verbGlossary.EXTENDS HOURS OF: {source} acceleration extends the daily operating hours of equipment to increase production and compress duration
verbGlossary.GATES: An inspection or commissioning resource must approve or witness a milestone before successor activities can proceed
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: A work calendar (weather days, pour windows, noise ordinances) governs when a weather-sensitive trade or material can be {target}d
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source}controls productive scheduling windows for trades and materials affected by weather or site conditions
verbGlossary.LEAD TIME FOR: {source}requires fabrication or procurement lead time that must precede {target} start
verbGlossary.LEVELED ACROSS: {source} leveling shifts non-critical activities so that a constrained resource (crane, labor crew, scaffolding) is not over-allocated across concurrent activities
verbGlossary.LOADED ON: {source} or equipment resource is loaded onto {target} at a specific quantity-per-day for each activity it supports
verbGlossary.MOBILIZES ADDITIONAL: A recovery {target} mobilizes additional crews or equipment beyond the original plan to regain lost time
verbGlossary.PACED BY: {source}is paced by a takt time cadence that moves crews through zones at a fixed production rate
verbGlossary.PACES: {source}planning paces {target} through sequential zones at a uniform cycle time to maintain continuous workflow
verbGlossary.PAIRED WITH: {source}is paired with {target} loading plan, ensuring operator availability matches machine scheduling
verbGlossary.RESOURCE-LOADS: {target} assigns resource quantities (headcount, equipment count) to activities to define the planned resource profile
verbGlossary.REVIEWS: {source} professional reviews the look-ahead {target} to verify safe work sequencing and required hold points
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED FOR: {source} crew is {target}d for specific activities in the CPM schedule with defined start and finish dates
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES: The look-ahead {target} confirms which trades are scheduled to work in the upcoming one-to-three-week window
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED BY: {source}activities are sequenced by {target} logic (FS, SS, FF relationships) relative to other trades' work
verbGlossary.SEQUENCES: Linear scheduling sequences equipment moves along a horizontal alignment (road, pipeline, tunnel) in production order
verbGlossary.SIMULATES: 4D/5D BIM scheduling simulates the construction sequence showing resource deployment over time in a 3D model
verbGlossary.SMOOTHS DEMAND FOR: {source} leveling smooths peak demand for {target} or equipment type by shifting float-available activities to reduce over-allocation
